nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings in lesson planning
Teacher professional autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I don't generally like to follow schedules imposed from the outside

nodes.label: Student engagement and learning
nodes.grounding_span: the human touch is very valuable in this realm for keeping students engaged

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for content creation
nodes.grounding_span: the time I was most impressed was when I asked it to give me a story very tailored to my student's specific job

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors in teaching materials

nodes.label: Preference for human-led ideation
nodes.grounding_span: I don't generally ask AI to plan my lessons

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI replacing teachers
nodes.grounding_span: I think it could potentially take over if AI developed more human simulating characteristics

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I sometimes go back and forth. For example, in a gap fill, I'll ask it to correct errors or change the mix

nodes.label: Selective delegation — prefers AI for content creation, retains control over structure
nodes.grounding_span: I'm more interested in having it create content than a structure
